title: Keyboard Shortcut Automation
concept: Teaching agents to use standard hotkeys (like Copy, Paste, Undo, Tab) to accelerate UI interactions.

exercises.prompt: What can VLMs help us do with screen images?

options.text: Identify places to click based on image details

exercises.explain: VLMs can analyze images to find exact locations where we need to click or drag!

cards.title: Keyboard Shortcut Automation
cards.body: Keyboard shortcuts, like Copy and Paste, help us do tasks faster without using the mouse!

cards.title: What are Keyboard Shortcuts?
cards.body: Keyboard shortcuts are combinations of keys that perform specific actions quickly on a computer. For example, pressing 'Ctrl+C' copies text.

cards.title: Like Superpowers for Your Fingers!
cards.body: Using keyboard shortcuts is like giving your fingers superpowers! You can accomplish tasks much faster, just like a superhero zipping through the city.

cards.title: Common Keyboard Shortcuts
cards.body:
Some common keyboard shortcuts include:
- Copy: Ctrl+C
- Paste: Ctrl+V
- Undo: Ctrl+Z
- Tab: Move to the next item.

cards.title: Myth: Shortcuts are Hard to Learn
cards.body: Some think keyboard shortcuts are too complicated, but once you know a few, they become quick and easy to use!

cards.title: Keyboard Shortcuts in Action
cards.body: A visual example of how using shortcuts can speed up our work. Compare the methods you can use!

exercises.prompt: What keyboard shortcut is used to undo an action?
exercises.explain: The 'Ctrl+Z' shortcut is the standard way to undo an action on most computers.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The keyboard shortcut for pasting is Ctrl+V.
exercises.explain: The correct keyboard shortcut for pasting items is indeed 'Ctrl+V'.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The keyboard shortcut to copy text is ______.
exercises.sentence: The keyboard shortcut to copy text is Ctrl+C.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'Ctrl+C', which is used to copy text.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a task you can do using a keyboard shortcut?
exercises.explain: You can use keyboard shortcuts to quickly copy text, making it much faster than using the mouse!

exercises.prompt: Which combination of tasks can you perform using keyboard shortcuts?

options.text: Copy text and paste it elsewhere

exercises.explain: Using keyboard shortcuts like 'Ctrl+C' to copy and 'Ctrl+V' to paste allows you to move text quickly, showing how effective shortcuts can be in accelerating your work!
